session_summary: Session pushing the deviance-bias result toward 'fully derived / fully pinned' for the (A)+(C) MAR term, with a standing cross-model verification gate. Marcus's role was to challenge the AI's framing that the (A)+(C) value was not load-bearing (it matters for LRT), audit whether the GPT-5.5 cross-check actually ran, stress-test the AI's MC-noise hypothesis by demanding a better simulation, enforce provenance/permanence on artifacts named 'scratch', and demand a multi-modality confidence verdict before handing off W1-W3 to a later session.

coded_turns.quote:
STANDING REQUIREMENT (user directive) — applies to ANY analytic derivation this session:
  independently cross-check it with GPT-5.5 (off the Claude lineage), reasoning_effort=xhigh
coded_turns.note: Resume handoff: targets the (A)+(C) value gap, imposes the GPT-5.5 cross-check gate, fences off deferred W1/W2/W3 work.

coded_turns.quote: What are you talking about it is not load bearing on the paper? It is directly relevant for liklihood ratio tests, no?
coded_turns.note: Rejects AI's claim that exact (A)+(C) value is not load-bearing; flags its relevance to LRT calibration.

coded_turns.quote: Background command "Run analytic Delta_KM closed form at n=800..6000 (deterministic)" completed (exit code 0)
coded_turns.note: System task-completion notification, not a human authored move.

coded_turns.quote: Background command "Run rem distribution / heavy-tail diagnostic at n=800,1500" completed (exit code 0)
coded_turns.note: System task-completion notification, not a human authored move.

coded_turns.quote: Background command "Run fitted-vs-oracle sign verification (R=4000, N=200, 4 cores)" completed (exit code 0)
coded_turns.note: System task-completion notification, not a human authored move.

coded_turns.quote: So did we never actually call Chat gpt-5.5?
coded_turns.note: Checks whether the mandated GPT-5.5 cross-model verification was actually executed, not just declared.

coded_turns.quote: right now your hypothesis is MC noise and you fit a new simulation and it was consistent with that, is that correct?
coded_turns.note: Probes for confirmation bias: did the AI form the MC-noise hypothesis then merely confirm it on a new sim.

coded_turns.quote: While this runs can we design a better simulation?
coded_turns.note: Demands a better (variance-reduced) simulation rather than accepting noisy MC point estimates.

coded_turns.quote: Background command "Launch paired higher-order variance-reduced simulation" completed (exit code 0)
coded_turns.note: System task-completion notification, not a human authored move.

coded_turns.quote: So are things reproducible. do we have the correct provenance, etc?
coded_turns.note: Checks reproducibility and provenance of the session's results before accepting them.

coded_turns.quote: I'm not a fan of the fact htat it is titled "scratch". If htese are central to the argument then then need to go somewhere permanent
coded_turns.note: Rejects 'scratch'-named scripts central to the argument; demands permanent, properly named homes.

coded_turns.quote: do we have strong evidence, from multiple sources, and multimodalities that we are comfortable claiming our bias derivation and corresponding correction are correct
coded_turns.note: Sets the bar for confidence: multi-source, multi-modality evidence before claiming the derivation correct.

coded_turns.quote: Adversarial per-pillar audit of the deviance-bias derivation + correction: is each claim warranted by committed evidence, across modalities?
coded_turns.note: System task-completion notification carrying the adversarial audit result, not a human authored move.

coded_turns.quote: Background command "Re-render after over-claim corrections" completed (exit code 0)
coded_turns.note: System task-completion notification, not a human authored move.

coded_turns.quote: Okay. So now we can shore up W1-W3 in another session?
coded_turns.note: Confirms theory is settled and defers W1-W3 application evidence to a separate next session.

coded_turns.quote: Give me the prompt I hand off tomorrow
coded_turns.note: Requests the resume handoff prompt for the next (W1-W3) session.

ai_side.evidence: I will *not* chase route (c) ... since the target is variance-limited and not load-bearing — but it remains available if you want a closed-form number.
ai_side.note: AI stated its route choice and put route (c) back to the human rather than silently dropping it.

ai_side.evidence: The one open item for *you*, not me, is the judgment call I flagged ... this revises a conclusion todo/018 had marked "settled,"
ai_side.note: AI flagged that its new finding overturns a previously-settled conclusion and asked for the human's context.

ai_side.evidence: That guess was **wrong**: it's a right tail ... the data overturned my first guess and forced the "imprecise / can't-distinguish" reading.
ai_side.note: AI self-corrected, admitting its initial heavy-left-tail hypothesis was wrong.

ai_side.evidence: it touches published source pointers and you may have a naming preference, so I'll do it on your say-so.
ai_side.note: AI deferred the rename of scratch scripts to the human's decision.

ai_side.evidence: Two items I deliberately did *not* touch ... the preregistered W1 sweep is noisier than the controlled run and its **H1 verdict is recorded FAIL**
ai_side.note: AI surfaced an unresolved FAIL verdict and an overclaim in the IC recovery framing as the human's call.

ai_side.evidence: **W3 (IC)** — calibrate "restores complete-data-equivalent selection" ... fix/retire the SB-arm bug (#1); decide on proper-MI engine-independence
ai_side.note: AI laid out the W1/W2/W3 next-session menu with specific options to address.
